metadata.paper_title: SSC CGL TI Similar Paper (Held on 12 Sep 2025 S3)

questions.question: If a % b = a² - b² and a % b = 63, find a and b.
questions.solution: Check each option: 8² - 1² = 64 - 1 = 63. So a=8, b=1.
questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.paper: SSC CGL TI Similar Paper (12 Sep 2025 S3)

questions.question: How many letters will remain unchanged if the word EDUCATION is arranged alphabetically?
questions.solution: EDUCATION → alphabetical: ACDEINOU T. No letter stays in same position. Answer: Zero.
questions.subject: Reasoning

questions.question: Select the letter-cluster that will replace (?): B, F, J, N, R, V, Z, ?
questions.solution: Each letter increases by +4. Z + 4 = D.

questions.question: Pointing to a boy, a woman said, 'He is the son of my father's only daughter.' How is the boy related to the woman?
questions.solution: My father's only daughter = herself. So the boy is her son.

questions.question: If '+' = '-' and '-' = '×', find: 12 + 4 - 3 = ?
questions.solution: Replace: 12 - 4 × 3 = 12 - 12 = 0 (BODMAS).
questions.subject: Reasoning

questions.question: Find the odd one out: A. 4:16:256  B. 3:9:81  C. 5:25:125  D. 6:36:1296
questions.solution: Pattern: n² = 2nd, (2nd)² = 3rd. 25² = 625 ≠ 125. So C is odd.
questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.paper: SSC CGL TI Similar Paper (12 Sep 2025 S3)

questions.question: Eight people P,Q,R,S,T,U,V,W sit in a circle. P is 2nd right of Q, R opposite P, S immediate left of R, T 3rd right of Q, U immediate left of S, W opposite T. Who is immediate right of Q?
questions.solution: From seating arrangement diagram, V is immediately right of Q.
questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.paper: SSC CGL TI Similar Paper (12 Sep 2025 S3)

questions.question: Find the missing term: ACE, EGI, IKM, MOQ, ?
questions.solution: Each group shifts +4. After MOQ: Q+4=U, S+4=W... wait pattern: QSU.
questions.subject: Reasoning

questions.question: What will come in place of (?): 2, 6, 18, 54, ?
questions.solution: Each term × 3: 54 × 3 = 162.

questions.question: 'apple is sweet fruit'='ka lo mi te', 'mango is sweet'='lo mi ra', 'apple mango fresh'='ka ra su'. How is 'sweet' coded?
questions.solution: Common in sentences 1&2: 'is sweet' = 'lo mi'. Common in 2&3: 'mango'='ra'. From sentence 2: 'is'='lo', so 'sweet'='mi'.
questions.subject: Reasoning

questions.question: The ratio of two numbers is 5:7 and their sum is 96. Find the numbers.
questions.solution: 5x+7x=96; x=8. Numbers: 40 and 56.

questions.question: If A is the brother of B, and B is the mother of C, how is A related to C?
questions.solution: A is B's brother, B is C's mother. So A is C's uncle.

questions.question: Order classical dance forms by earliest documented mentions (earliest first): 1.Bharatanatyam 2.Kathak 3.Mohiniyattam 4.Manipuri
questions.solution: Bharatanatyam (Natya Shastra ~200BCE), Kathak (Bhakti movement), Manipuri (medieval), Mohiniyattam (16th-17th century).

questions.question: The Tutsa tribe of Arunachal Pradesh observes the Pongtu Festival mainly to invoke blessings from which god prior to millet sowing?
questions.solution: Pongtu Festival is observed to seek blessings from supreme deity Rangkhothak before millet sowing.

questions.question: Assertion (A): Iron and steel qualifies as a heavy industry. Reason (R): It uses lightweight inputs such as cotton and rubber.

questions.solution: Iron and steel is heavy industry (A true), but uses heavy inputs like iron ore, coal, limestone — not cotton/rubber (R false).

questions.question: Convert passive to active: 'It has been claimed that important data was being disclosed by employees within the firm.'

options.A: Someone has claimed that employees were disclosing important data within the firm.
options.B: Important data had been disclosed by employees, it is claimed.
options.C: Employees have allegedly been disclosing important data within the firm.
options.D: The firm was allegedly disclosing employee's data.

questions.solution: Impersonal passive → active: Someone has claimed that employees were disclosing important data within the firm.
questions.subject: English

questions.question: Rearrange sentences: 1.Team examined feedback. 2.Findings applied to enhance customer journey. 3.Focused promotional campaign designed. 4.Information gathered through multiple sources.
questions.solution: Collect data → examine → design campaign → apply findings. Order: 4-1-3-2.
questions.subject: English

questions.question: Convert to direct speech: 'The manager told the staff that due dates must be met strictly.'

options.A: "Due dates must be met strictly," said the manager.
options.B: "You should meet the due dates strictly," said the manager.
options.C: "Meet the due dates strictly," the manager said.
options.D: "Due dates are to be met," said the manager.

questions.solution: Statement with that-clause. Must remains must. Direct: "Due dates must be met strictly," said the manager.
questions.subject: English
questions.paper: SSC CGL TI Similar Paper (12 Sep 2025 S3)

questions.question: Select sentence containing the homonym of 'bailey' (castle courtyard): 'The researcher cited a bailey in Norman defensive design.'

options.A: Bailey presented the findings on Norman fortifications at the seminar.
options.B: The outer bailey encircled the motte and was guarded by watchtowers.
options.C: The soldiers retreated into the bailey when the main gate collapsed.
options.D: A wooden palisade was raised around the bailey for extra protection.

questions.solution: In option A, 'Bailey' is a proper noun (person's name) — same pronunciation, different meaning. That's a homonym.
questions.subject: English
questions.paper: SSC CGL TI Similar Paper (12 Sep 2025 S3)

questions.question: What is the main point of groupthink as presented in the passage?
questions.solution: Groupthink = team's hunger for agreement becoming stronger than willingness to evaluate critically.

questions.question: What remedy does the passage recommend for groupthink and conformity?

questions.question: Choose suitable option to replace highlighted part: 'No one knows where is he staying.'

questions.question: Choose suitable option to replace: 'She demanded me to swallow the medication.'

questions.question: Rearrange to form coherent paragraph: 1.Influences include sunlight, atmosphere, orbital path. 2.Long-term climate shifts shaped by natural and human-driven influences. 3.Most prominent human influence: greenhouse gases. 4.Together, these control planet's energy balance.
questions.solution: Intro (2) → natural examples (1) → human factor (3) → conclusion (4). Order: 2,1,3,4.

questions.question: Find error: 'The higher the office was (1)/ the more pressing became the need (2)/ for prudence, propriety, and (3)/ avoiding of early disclosure. (4)'
questions.solution: Error in part 4: 'avoiding of' is incorrect. Should be 'avoidance of' to maintain parallelism with prudence, propriety.

questions.question: Find error: 'The report's focus on tech-utopianism, though praiseworthy, (1)/ ignore the deep-rooted inequities (2)/ that continue in data availability (3)/ across demographic and regional gaps. (4)'

questions.question: Select correct option: 'Her analysis was __ than impressive; it was nearly revelatory.'
questions.solution: 'More than impressive' = exceeding the level of impressive. Correct fixed expression.
questions.subject: English
questions.paper: SSC CGL TI Similar Paper (12 Sep 2025 S3)

questions.question: Convert to indirect speech: He said, 'I am not feeling fine.'

options.A: He said that he is not feeling fine.
options.B: He said that he was not feeling fine.
options.C: He said that he didn't feel fine.
options.D: He said he not feeling fine.

questions.solution: Reporting verb 'said' (past) → backshift: 'am not feeling' → 'was not feeling'. Pronoun I→he.
questions.subject: English
questions.paper: SSC CGL TI Similar Paper (12 Sep 2025 S3)

questions.question: One-word substitute for: 'A condition of disuse or inactivity.'
questions.solution: Desuetude = state of disuse; something no longer used or inactive due to long neglect.
questions.subject: English
questions.paper: SSC CGL TI Similar Paper (12 Sep 2025 S3)

questions.question: Change active to passive: 'Why did the editor disregard the major stylistic irregularities?'

options.A: Why have the major stylistic irregularities been disregarded by the editor?
options.B: Why are the major stylistic irregularities being disregarded by the editor?
options.C: Why did the major stylistic irregularities be disregarded by the editor?
options.D: Why were the major stylistic irregularities disregarded by the editor?

questions.solution: Simple past passive. Plural subject 'irregularities' → 'were disregarded by the editor'.
questions.subject: English
